display.purpose: Brand wordmark and page H1s; Lora serif.

headline.purpose: Section titles, each led by a coral tick.

figure.purpose: The hero statistic on a KPI detail page; Lora, tabular nums.

body.purpose: All prose; Inter, held to a 64ch measure.

shadows.name: card-rest
shadows.purpose: Barely-there seam under a resting surface.

shadows.name: lift-hover
shadows.purpose: Hover/active state for interactive cards; the only real depth in the system.

motion.name: card-transition
motion.value: transform 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease
motion.purpose: Card hover lift and border-color shift.

motion.name: rise
motion.purpose: Entrance for sections and cards; staggered 50ms across the KPI grid. Gated behind prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference.

components.name: KPI Card
components.description: The primary navigation unit on the overview — a linked card showing a KPI title, its latest figure, a year-over-year delta, and a sparkline.
components.html:
Taux de frais administratifs

8,4 %▼ 0,3 pt
données jusqu’à 2024vs moyenne OCDE
components.css: .ds-kpi-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.75rem; min-height: 200px; padding: 1.5rem; background: var(--paper, #fbfaf6); border: 1px solid var(--rule, #e2dccd); border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(40,38,31,0.04); text-decoration: none; color: var(--ink, #28261f); transition: transform 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease; } .ds-kpi-card:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); border-color: var(--coral, #cc785c); box-shadow: 0 12px 30px -12px rgba(40,38,31,0.22); } .ds-kpi-card__title { font-family: Lora, Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.12rem; font-weight: 600; } .ds-kpi-card__figure { display: flex; align-items: baseline; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.5rem 0.75rem; } .ds-kpi-card__value { font-family: Lora, Georgia, serif; font-size: 2.1rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.02em; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: var(--ink, #28261f); } .ds-kpi-card__yoy { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.35em; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.82rem; font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-yoy--good { color: #2c6e49; } .ds-kpi-card__asof { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.7rem; color: #8a8576; } .ds-kpi-card__badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.45em; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.7rem; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #8a8576; } .ds-kpi-card__badge::before { content: ''; width: 1.1rem; height: 0; border-top: 2px dashed #8a8576; }

components.name: Stat Callout
components.description: The hero figure on a KPI detail page. Paper surface with a 4px Republic-Bleu left edge — sanctioned because it is a data signal (France's figure), not decoration.
components.html: 110,6 %en 2024▲ 2,1 pt sur un an
components.css: .ds-stat-callout { display: flex; align-items: baseline; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.75rem 1.5rem; padding: 1.5rem 2rem; background: var(--paper, #fbfaf6); border: 1px solid var(--rule, #e2dccd); border-left: 4px solid var(--bleu, #0055a4); border-radius: 12px; } .ds-stat-callout__value { font-family: Lora, Georgia, serif; font-size: clamp(2.6rem, 6vw, 3.6rem); font-weight: 600; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: -0.03em; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: var(--ink, #28261f); } .ds-stat-callout__year { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.95rem; color: #8a8576; } .ds-stat-callout__yoy { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.95rem; font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-yoy--bad { color: #b3261e; }

components.name: Last-updated Pill
components.description: A rounded metadata chip in the masthead showing the data freshness date.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 0.74rem; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #8a8576; background: var(--paper, #fbfaf6); border: 1px solid var(--rule, #e2dccd); border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.35em 0.85em; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Methodology Disclosure
components.description: A collapsible 'Méthodologie & sources' panel — the trust mechanism, available on every KPI. Coral summary with a rotating caret.
components.html:
Méthodologie & sources
Ratio des dépenses de fonctionnement sur les dépenses totales (COFOG, base 2020). Source : INSEE, comptes des administrations publiques.
components.css: .ds-methodology { border: 1px solid var(--rule, #e2dccd); border-radius: 12px; background: var(--paper, #fbfaf6); } .ds-methodology__summary { list-style: none; cursor: pointer; padding: 1rem 1.5rem; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; color: #a8553a; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.6em; } .ds-methodology__summary::-webkit-details-marker { display: none; } .ds-methodology__summary::before { content: '\25b8'; color: var(--coral, #cc785c); transition: transform 0.18s ease; } .ds-methodology[open] .ds-methodology__summary::before { transform: rotate(90deg); } .ds-methodology__body { padding: 0 1.5rem 1rem; color: #514c40; font-size: 0.95rem; max-width: 64ch; }

components.name: Inline Link
components.description: The primary interaction affordance in a read-only dashboard. Coral-ink text with a thin underline; hover shifts to ink.
components.css: .ds-link { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; color: #a8553a; text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-thickness: 1px; text-underline-offset: 2px; } .ds-link:hover { color: var(--ink, #28261f); } .ds-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--coral, #cc785c); outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: 3px; }

narrative.overview: Fiscoscope reads like an honest civic instrument, not an argument. The system is built on editorial restraint: a warm cream canvas, a Lora serif for figures and headings, an Inter sans for prose, and color spent almost entirely on meaning. The data is the protagonist; the interface is the quiet, well-set page it sits on. The single most distinctive decision is a strict separation of roles for color: the French tricolore is reserved for data semantics (bleu is the France series, rouge marks meaningful negatives only) while coral carries all interaction. Color never decorates; if it appears, it means something. The system rejects the government-portal look, partisan coloring, the generic SaaS dashboard, and crypto/fintech flash.
keyCharacteristics: Warm cream editorial canvas (#f4f1ea), never stark white, paired with a serif — a committed identity choice, not the AI sand-beige cliché.
Lora serif for every figure and heading; Inter for body. Contrast by axis (serif × sans).
Tricolore = data only; coral = interaction only. The two never cross.
Flat surfaces at rest, hairline borders, lift only on hover.
Tabular numerals wherever figures are read; a 64ch measure for prose.

rules.name: The Tricolore-Is-Data Rule
rules.body: Bleu and rouge belong to the data layer and nowhere else. Coral owns interaction. If you are reaching for blue or red to style a button, a header, or a card, you are wrong — use coral or ink. The flag is never decoration.

rules.name: The Color-Earns-Its-Place Rule
rules.body: On any given screen, meaningful color covers a small fraction of the surface. The default state of everything is ink-on-cream. Rarity is what makes a colored mark legible.

rules.name: The Serif-For-Numbers Rule
rules.body: Every figure the reader is meant to weigh is set in Lora with tabular numerals. Numbers carry the editorial authority; sans is for the words around them.

rules.name: The One-Measure Rule
rules.body: Prose never exceeds the 64ch measure. A wide column of body text is the government-portal tell.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest, defined by tone and a 1px rule. Shadow is earned by interaction, never applied for decoration.

dos: Do keep bleu and rouge for the data layer only; style all interaction with coral (#cc785c / Coral Ink #a8553a for text).
Do set every reader-facing figure in Lora with tabular numerals.
Do keep surfaces flat at rest and let depth appear only on hover (0 12px 30px -12px rgba(40,38,31,0.22) + coral border).
Do pair color encodings with a second cue — the OECD line is dashed, not just taupe — so charts survive color-vision deficiency.
Do hold prose to the 64ch measure and use the cream canvas (#f4f1ea), never stark white.
Do disclose breaks and caveats in a rouge-ruled note below the chart, not hidden.
donts: Don't look like a government portal: no dense undifferentiated tables, no full-width prose, no low-trust clutter.
Don't read as partisan or activist: no flag-waving chrome, no outrage callouts, no editorializing color.
Don't fall into the generic SaaS dashboard: no hero-metric template, no identical icon-card grids, no gradient text.
Don't add crypto/fintech flash: no dark-neon theme, no animated tickers, no hype motion.
Don't use a border-left/border-right colored stripe as decoration. The only sanctioned thick edge is the 4px bleu stat-callout edge, because it is a data signal.
Don't apply shadow to a resting surface, and don't put a heavy shadow anywhere — it reads as a 2014 app.
Don't introduce a third type family or pair two similar sans; the system is Lora × Inter, full stop.
